steps.title: Check if Function I displays its maximum value
steps.content:
Function I is $f(x) = 16(1.25)^x + 43$. Since $1.25 \gt 1$, the exponential term $(1.25)^x$ increases without bound as $x$ increases.

This means $f(x)$ has no maximum value on the domain $x \geq 0$ — it approaches infinity.

Therefore, neither the constant 43 nor the coefficient 16 represents a maximum value.

steps.title: Check if Function II displays its maximum value
steps.content:
Function II is $g(x) = 6(0.75)^x$. Since $0 \lt 0.75 \lt 1$, the exponential term $(0.75)^x$ decreases as $x$ increases.

The maximum value occurs at $x = 0$: $g(0) = 6(0.75)^0 = 6$.

As $x$ increases beyond 0, $(0.75)^x$ decreases toward 0, so $g(x)$ decreases toward 0.

The maximum value of Function II is 6, and this maximum is displayed directly as the coefficient in the equation.

That matches Choice B — II only.
